parent_message.text: Morning Direct team! My customer Rudy's launched online ordering at 1x location on Thursday last week and have generated 50+ orders in that time! There are only 2x customer (myself and their marketing manager) in the customer database despite deactivating Guest Checkout in the feature settings. Is there anything they are doing wrong or can do in the future to ensure customer data collection from each order?

messages.text: can you share accountID ?
Sure it's 64b67008a33ee1f65c3628f3

Hey Richard Hyde, Customer information is populated in the CRM when orders reach a finalized state (such as delivered, finalized, or cancelled etc). I checked the dashboard, and it appears that none of the orders are currently in a finalized state — they are all either in `pickup ready` or `accepted` state.

Hi team !
Quick question - a (future) 12 loc customer is asking this :
"Today, we created a "service fee" product with a fixed amount, automatically added to the cart by default.
 Would it be possible to implement this service fee system on Direct as well?
 And eventually, could we consider a version where the fee is proportional to the order amount?"

--> Is that a possibility we have in Direct ?

messages.text: + what will be the pricing of the Mobile App ?
Andy Soh
Hi Anouk
Service fee is already available on Direct
As a %
You can set it up on the direct dashboard> locations > delivery or pickup > settings

Thanks Ben!
And for the pricing Mobile App ? Not included in the 69€ Direct package right ?
Thank you !

parent_message.text: Hey team, where does the information pull from when a customer selects cities on the dropdown for pick up? We can see some errors here and can't see where to adjust. Thanks cc Nadya Williams

messages.text:
Hi Jay Stewart
We get this information at the time of provisioning a channel Link, In order to fix this we would need to adjust them in locations addresses in frontend.deliverect.com
and then sync the outlets from social-frontend.deliverect.com
Thanks Puneet, that worked. Cheers

parent_message.text: One more question :grimacing: It seems that order number 9347167 was placed 31 minutes after the items were listed as unavailable (11:01), even though the category availability was correctly set for 8:00 - 10:30. My assumption is that the customer added the items to their basket during the availability time, but took longer to place the order when the categories were no longer available. Is there a way to confirm that ?
